title: Cryptool vs OTCMarsBase: Trading Token Allocations and SAFTs OTC (2026)
author: Cryptool
excerpt_html: OTCMarsBase is an OTC desk for buying and selling token deals, allocations, and SAFTs. Cryptool has the same OTC marketplace built in, but connected to the rest of your stack, so a position you rai...
content_html:
OTCMarsBase is an OTC desk for buying and selling token deals, allocations, and SAFTs. Cryptool has the same OTC marketplace built in, but connected to the rest of your stack, so a position you raised, hold, or are vesting can be listed and sold without exporting it to a separate desk. Put simply, OTCMarsBase is the desk, and Cryptool is the desk plus the fund around it.

At a glance

  • Core focus: Cryptool is an all-in-one platform (Portfolio, Raise, Invest, Marketplace). OTCMarsBase focuses on OTC deal trading.
  • OTC allocations and SAFTs: both support buying and selling allocations and SAFTs.
  • Post-TGE and locked positions: Cryptool supports listing remaining presale, IDO-locked, and post-TGE positions.
  • Connected to your portfolio: in Cryptool a listing can come straight from a position you already track or raised.
  • Private and broker listings: Cryptool supports public, private, and broker-facilitated listings.
  • The wider stack: Cryptool also covers raising, co-investment, and portfolio reporting.

Where Cryptool wins

  • The marketplace is not a silo. A standalone OTC desk means moving positions in and out of yet another tool. In Cryptool, the allocations you raised or hold are already there, so listing and selling is one step inside the same platform.
  • It covers the full position lifecycle. Raise the allocation, track its vesting, then list and sell it when you want liquidity, all in one place.
  • Flexible listing types. Public, private, and broker-managed listings, with anonymous options, suit both individual sellers and groups.

Where OTCMarsBase is strong

OTCMarsBase is a focused OTC venue with a dedicated deal-trading audience. If your only activity is OTC trading of token deals and you have no need for raising, portfolio tracking, or fund management, a specialist desk can serve that single purpose well.

Which should you choose?

Choose OTCMarsBase if OTC deal trading is all you do and you want a standalone desk. Choose Cryptool if you want that same OTC marketplace connected to the raise, portfolio, and fund management you already run, so positions move from raise to hold to sale without leaving the platform.

FAQ

Can I sell SAFTs and locked allocations on Cryptool?

Yes. Cryptool's marketplace supports allocations, SAFTs, and post-TGE or IDO-locked positions, with public, private, and broker listings.

Is the marketplace connected to the rest of Cryptool?

Yes. Positions you raised or hold in Cryptool can be listed directly, rather than exported to a separate desk.

Does Cryptool only do OTC?

No. The marketplace is one module alongside Portfolio, Raise, and Invest.

See how Cryptool's OTC marketplace works at cryptool.io.
